Added regression coverage in `src/shared/api/index.test.ts` for both fallback-success and fallback-unavailable paths. Verified with `pnpm exec vitest run src/shared/api/index.test.ts` and `pnpm build`. |
| EX-150 | Resolve merge conflicts from the latest `origin/i23` sync. | COMPLETED | Reconciled `ROADMAP.md` conflict markers, preserved both branches’ completed entries, and renumbered conflicting IDs to keep the roadmap sequence unique. |
| EX-151 | Mitigate slow home “Latest Transactions” rendering under high `/v2/explorer/transactions` latency. | COMPLETED | 2026-03-05 probe still shows production skew (`/v2/explorer/blocks?per_page=5` ~`0.08s` vs `/v2/explorer/transactions?per_page=5` often `4s`-`11s`). Frontend mitigation shipped in `src/widgets/latest-transactions/LatestTransactions.vue`: render path now merges optimistic SSE rows + fetched rows + bounded local cache, writes cache snapshots from successful all-status fetches, and suppresses redundant SSE-triggered refetch while a fetch is pending. Added tested helper module `src/widgets/latest-transactions/model.ts` + `model.test.ts`, plus SSE regression `tests/e2e/latest-transactions-stream.smoke.test.ts` asserting streamed tx row renders before slow fetch completion. Verified with `pnpm exec vitest run src/widgets/latest-transactions/model.test.ts src/widgets/latest-transactions/LatestTransactions.test.ts tests/e2e/latest-transactions-stream.smoke.test.ts` and `pnpm typecheck`. |
| EX-152 | Restore `/transactions/:hash` detail usability when filtered instruction history is temporarily empty and decode nested multisig custom payloads into readable metadata. | COMPLETED | Added instruction-history fallback in `src/shared/ui/components/InstructionsTable.vue`: when `/v2/explorer/instructions?transaction_hash=...` temporarily returns empty, the table now probes detail endpoints (`/instructions/{hash}/{index}`) via `src/shared/lib/instruction-fallback.ts` and renders recovered rows (including deep-link priority index probes). Added multisig custom payload enrichment in `src/shared/lib/multisig-custom.ts` and wired it into `InstructionsTable` so nested `Custom` multisig envelopes now expose readable summary metadata (`variant/account/instructions_count/decoded instruction hints`) in the JSON pane while preserving raw payload. Added regression coverage in `src/shared/lib/instruction-fallback.test.ts`, `src/shared/lib/multisig-custom.test.ts`, and `src/shared/ui/components/InstructionsTable.test.ts` (fallback integration). Verified with `pnpm exec vitest run src/shared/lib/instruction-fallback.test.ts src/shared/lib/multisig-custom.test.ts src/shared/ui/components/InstructionsTable.test.ts`, `pnpm typecheck`, and `pnpm build`. |
| EX-153 | Stabilize `/transactions` list under slow Torii responses (faster first paint + less jumpy SSE refresh). | COMPLETED | `src/shared/ui/components/TransactionsTable.vue` now hydrates the default unfiltered list from a bounded local cache (`transactions_table_cache_v1`) for faster first paint, merges optimistic stream rows with fetched/cache rows on page 1, suppresses duplicate stream-hash reloads, and queues at most one SSE-triggered refetch while a request is already pending (avoids stacked reload races/jumps on slow Torii responses). Expanded regression coverage in `src/shared/ui/components/TransactionsTable.test.ts` for cache hydration, pending-refetch queueing, and duplicate-stream suppression. Verified with `pnpm exec vitest run src/shared/ui/components/TransactionsTable.test.ts` and `pnpm typecheck`. |
| EX-154 | Stabilize home latest block/transaction freshness so refreshes stop flipping between stale and near-head snapshots. | COMPLETED | Failover selection in `src/shared/api/index.ts` now probes all resolved candidates and chooses the freshest healthy head (highest block height, then newest timestamp) instead of first-match; built-in `Nexus/Testus` presets were removed from automatic failover candidate resolution so cross-network auto-switches no longer happen implicitly. Explorer GETs now use `cache: 'no-store'` (`get()` + `/peers`) to avoid stale browser-cache snapshots after refresh. Updated docs/sample config (`docs/roadmap/torii_outage_failover_runbook.md`, `public/config.json.example`) and expanded API regression coverage in `src/shared/api/index.test.ts` (freshest-candidate preference, no preset auto-probe, no-store fetch option). Verified with `pnpm exec vitest run src/shared/api/index.test.ts` and `pnpm typecheck`. |
| EX-155 | Add explicit home-widget freshness badges so latest blocks/transactions expose sample age and stale/fresh state. | COMPLETED | Added shared freshness helper `src/shared/lib/freshness.ts` (`fresh <= 15s`, `delayed <= 60s`, else `stale`) and wired home widgets to show `Sample age` + tone chips via existing telemetry i18n keys in `src/widgets/latest-blocks/LatestBlocks.vue` and `src/widgets/latest-transactions/LatestTransactions.vue`. Added regression coverage in `src/shared/lib/freshness.test.ts`, `src/widgets/latest-blocks/LatestBlocks.test.ts`, and `src/widgets/latest-transactions/LatestTransactions.test.ts` (stale + unknown badge states). Verified with `pnpm exec vitest run src/shared/lib/freshness.test.ts src/widgets/latest-blocks/LatestBlocks.test.ts src/widgets/latest-transactions/LatestTransactions.test.ts` and `pnpm typecheck`. |

Verified with `pnpm exec vitest run src/shared/runtime-config.test.ts src/shared/api/index.test.ts src/shared/api/schemas.test.ts`, `pnpm typecheck`, and `pnpm build`. |

| EX-206 | Replace Kotodama Studio’s preview/endpoint compile flow with a browser-local TypeScript compiler that emits chain-valid IVM artifacts for the Studio language subset. | COMPLETED | 2026-03-28 completed: removed the remote compiler/preview fallback from `src/shared/lib/kotodama-studio-deploy.ts` and `/studio`, added a browser-local TypeScript compiler in `src/shared/lib/kotodama-studio-compiler.ts` that emits `IVM` + `CNTR` + `LTLB` artifacts for the Studio-generated Kotodama subset, switched hashing to pure-JS BLAKE2b via `@noble/hashes`, and hard-gated deploy on real local diagnostics instead of preview state. Added regression coverage in `src/shared/lib/kotodama-studio-compiler.test.ts`, updated `src/shared/lib/kotodama-studio-deploy.test.ts` and `src/pages/KotodamaStudio.test.ts`, and verified with `pnpm typecheck`, `pnpm exec vitest run src/shared/lib/kotodama-studio-compiler.test.ts src/shared/lib/kotodama-studio-deploy.test.ts src/pages/KotodamaStudio.test.ts`, and `pnpm build`. This is intentionally limited to the Studio-emitted language subset, not full upstream Kotodama parity. |
| EX-207 | Build a full browser-local TypeScript Kotodama compiler with parser, semantic analysis, manifest generation, and bytecode parity against the upstream Rust compiler. | COMPLETED | 2026-03-28 started: audited upstream `../iroha/crates/kotodama_lang` lexer/parser/semantic/compiler modules and reset the roadmap to distinguish Studio-subset compilation from full-language parity. Follow-up compiler slices broadened `src/shared/lib/kotodama-studio-compiler.ts` to lower local bindings, local reassignment, query-result bindings, `while` loops, integer arithmetic expressions (`+ - * / %`), underscored integer literals, unary negation, and less blockly-specific `if` / `while` condition syntax without mandatory parentheses or `else`. Subsequent work added typed helper functions and entrypoints with params/returns, inline call lowering, `return` statements, ternary expressions, both `name: Type` and `Type name` parameter syntax, `poseidon2`, `assert`/`assert_eq`, `mint_asset`, and `burn_asset`, plus far-jump-friendly `if` / `while` / ternary lowering so larger inlined helper bodies still compile inside the browser. A later slice added boolean/logical control-flow support for unary `!`, `&&`, `||`, and loop-control statements `break` / `continue`. Another tranche added tuple type parsing, tuple literals, tuple params/returns, numeric tuple member access (`.0`, `.1`, ...), recursive tuple binding lowering for inline calls and returns, and embedded `CNTR` param/return type metadata strings. A later tranche added parsed `struct` declarations, resolved struct type references, struct-constructor calls (`StructName(...)`), named member access on structs, nested aggregate lowering across tuples/structs, and unknown-field diagnostics. The next tranche added upstream-style top-level `#[access(...)]` attribute skipping, `state Name: Map<K, V>` parsing, indexed durable map reads and writes (`State[key]`), `contains`, `get_or_insert_default`, `isqrt`, optional assert messages, and real durable-path lowering through `BUILD_PATH_MAP_KEY`, `BUILD_PATH_KEY_NORITO`, `POINTER_TO_NORITO`, and `POINTER_FROM_NORITO` syscalls so `irohaswap`-style contracts now compile in the browser. A later tranche added call-style pointer constructors used by upstream samples (`account_id(...)`, `asset_definition(...)`, `name(...)`, `json(...)`, `blob(...)`, `norito_bytes(...)`), non-mutating durable-map `get_or(...)`, `current_time_ms()`, and additional arithmetic parity for `abs`, `min`, `max`, `div_ceil`, `gcd`, and `mean`. The latest tranche adds trigger/runtime builtin coverage for `trigger_event()`, `resolve_account_alias(...)`, `execute_instruction(...)`, and JSON field getters `json_get_int/json_get_json/json_get_name/json_get_account_id/json_get_asset_definition_id`, matching the upstream syscall surface well enough for trigger-bridge helper contracts to compile locally in the browser. A follow-up slice now adds typed local `let map: Map<K, V> = Map::new()` bindings, local-map aliasing, one-slot in-memory `contains` / `get_or` / `get_or_insert_default` lowering, indexed local-map reads and writes, and explicit diagnostics when `Map::new()` lacks a declared map type or a local map is used without indexing. The newest metadata tranche now parses function-level `#[access(...)]` annotations instead of skipping them, derives durable-state read/write keys transitively across helper-function calls for the browser-supported subset, emits contract-level `access_set_hints`, fills entrypoint `read_keys` / `write_keys` / `access_hints_complete` / `access_hints_skipped`, and embeds declared state descriptors into the `CNTR` section so unused state still survives as artifact metadata. A trigger-follow-up broadened top-level trigger syntax to accept the upstream `trigger` alias, `call namespace::entrypoint`, `repeats`, and `metadata { ... }` blocks for the browser-supported subset, embeds those trigger descriptors into `CNTR` entrypoint metadata instead of leaving `triggers: []`, and adds a semantic-style diagnostic when a trigger targets a non-public helper. The next trigger-metadata slice corrected embedded Norito enum/payload wrapping for trigger descriptors, added trigger `authority` encoding, lowered supported `on data ...` and `on pipeline ...` filters into `EventFilterBox` metadata, and emits explicit diagnostics for unsupported `rwa` data triggers and non-canonical trigger authority literals. The newest manifest/access tranche now exports compiler-derived manifest metadata directly from the browser compiler, feeds that structure through `src/shared/lib/kotodama-studio-deploy.ts` instead of rebuilding a downgraded summary manifest in the UI, and upgrades dynamic host-effect access hints so `transfer_asset`, `mint_asset`, `burn_asset`, `set_account_detail`, and `execute_instruction(...)` conservatively emit `*` read/write wildcard coverage instead of incomplete skipped-hint metadata. A 2026-03-29 follow-up landed bounded Norito literal access decoding in the browser compiler so static `execute_instruction(norito_bytes(...))` payloads can now derive exact `domain` / `asset_def` / `nft` / `trigger` detail keys plus `role` / `perm.role` and `trigger.repetitions` effects where the payload is fully decodable, while `execute_query(...)` now derives bounded asset-definition reads where possible and otherwise falls back to upstream-style wildcard coverage instead of reporting a skipped hint. A later 2026-03-29 slice moved canonical single-key i105 rendering into `src/shared/lib/account-literal.ts` and expanded static literal access decoding to cover account-targeted `set/remove_key_value`, `grant/revoke` permission and role bindings, and `FindAssetById` asset-bucket queries, including dataspace-scoped asset IDs, so exact `account`, `account.detail`, `perm.account`, `role.binding`, and `asset` keys are emitted when the payload is fully decodable. The next 2026-03-29 tranche adds transparent `SetAssetKeyValue` / `RemoveAssetKeyValue` Norito envelope decoding, derives exact `asset.detail` metadata access for static asset-balance edits, widens `FindAssetById` query hints to include the same bounded related reads the browser compiler can recover (`account`, `asset`, `asset_def`, synthetic `domain:aid`), and corrects metadata `set/remove_key_value` hints for `domain` / `account` / `asset_def` / `nft` so their detail keys are recorded as read-write instead of write-only. The newest 2026-03-29 slice now decodes static boxed `iroha.transfer`, `iroha.mint`, and `iroha.burn` payloads for the supported asset/domain/asset-definition/NFT/trigger variants, deriving exact bounded access for domain transfers, asset-definition transfers, numeric asset transfers, NFT transfers, asset mint/burn, and trigger-repetition mint/burn instead of collapsing those fully decodable `execute_instruction(norito_bytes(...))` calls to wildcard host effects. Expanded regression coverage in `src/shared/lib/account-literal.test.ts` and `src/shared/lib/kotodama-studio-compiler.test.ts` now covers canonical public-key-to-i105 rendering, malformed/unsupported multihash rejection, exact account detail access, exact account grant/revoke access, exact account-scoped asset query access, exact transparent asset-metadata instruction access, exact boxed transfer/mint/burn access, the map-state success path, constructor/builtin success cases, trigger/runtime builtin success, explicit access metadata, state descriptor embedding, access-attribute parse errors, non-map index diagnostics, local-map success and failure paths, pointer-map `get_or` default errors, illegal value use of `execute_instruction(...)`, trigger metadata embedding, trigger authority/data/pipeline metadata success and failure cases, compiler-exported manifest metadata, wildcard host-access hints, exact decoded instruction/query access-hint cases, and non-public trigger target diagnostics; `src/shared/lib/kotodama-studio-deploy.test.ts` now also verifies the deploy draft consumes compiler-derived manifest data. Reverified the affected compiler/deploy/studio regression slices with `pnpm exec vitest run src/shared/lib/kotodama-studio-compiler.test.ts src/shared/lib/kotodama-studio-deploy.test.ts src/pages/KotodamaStudio.test.ts` and targeted ESLint on the touched compiler files. A later pass restored repository-wide `pnpm typecheck --pretty false` and `pnpm build`; the final 2026-05-31 audit also passed `pnpm run typecheck` and `pnpm run build` against the reusable SDK package boundary. 2026-05-31 completion audit: EX-207 is closed. The compiler now lives in the upstream reusable JS SDK package at `../iroha/javascript/iroha_js/src/kotodamaCompiler`, Explorer consumes it through the `@iroha/iroha-js/kotodama-compiler` package boundary, all current upstream `.ko` corpus sources are represented by refreshable Rust fixtures, expected drift lists are empty, successful fixtures match Rust manifests, debug rows, `CNTR`/`LTLB` sections, and full artifacts byte-for-byte, and upstream SDK tests cover the tracked corpus plus package-dist imports. Remaining future Kotodama language expansion should be opened as new roadmap items when upstream adds new contracts or APIs. |

Fixed two client-side reactive feedback loops: `src/pages/KotodamaStudio.vue` now persists only metadata/workspace/workflow changes instead of mutating a deep-watched document inside its own callback, and `src/features/studio/WorkflowStudioCanvas.vue` now suppresses no-op `v-model` echoes by comparing normalized workflow snapshots before syncing or emitting.
